prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is shown in black and white and appears to center on a car and a car-wax product.

At the beginning, the frame shows a dark, shiny automobile viewed from the front. The car is parked outdoors on what looks like a driveway or path, with bushes or trees around it. The chrome grille and headlights are prominent, and the hood reflects light, suggesting a polished surface.

A later shot introduces the product visually: a large can labeled “instant instant J-wax” appears in the foreground, partially blocking the view of the same front-facing car behind it. The can is much larger than the car in the frame, suggesting either a close-up product shot or a superimposed/oversized product image. The product name “J-wax” is very large and central, with “instant” repeated above it.

By the final frame, the scene has changed to a side view of a long, dark car parked outdoors near a curb or driveway in a leafy residential or park-like setting. A very large can sits on top of or is visually placed above the car. The can reads “NEW! from Johnson’s Wax” along the top and “J-wax” in large letters across the side. The car’s glossy finish and bright highlights are visually emphasized. The final composition strongly links the shiny car with the J-wax product.
